changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_enabled to k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_configured for consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to continuous monitoring through audit logging, network policies, and admission controllers. The original function names were mostly correct but required slight adjustments for consistency with naming conventions. The term 'configured' is more appropriate than 'enabled' for monitoring aspects, aligning with the descriptive nature of the checks.
naming_improvements: Use 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for monitoring-related functions to better describe the setup and configuration aspect.
title: Continuous Monitoring - Ongoing monitoring of system and...
step1_reasoning: Continuous monitoring in the cloud often involves tracking changes to network configurations and enabling security services. In Kubernetes, equivalent monitoring can be achieved through audit logging to track changes to the cluster state and configurations. NetworkPolicies can be monitored to ensure they are enforced and changes are logged. Admission controllers can be configured to monitor and enforce policies on resource creation and updates.
